title: cousinia kotschyi subsp. khansarica
intro: Cousinia kotschyi subsp. khansarica, also known as Khansari Cousinia, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family, native to Iran. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including wound healing, anti-aging, and protection against cancer cells, as well as showing potential in the treatment of certain bacterial infections. Herbal preparations of Cousinia kotschyi subsp. khansarica include infusion, decoction, and tincture, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
